title: Add backoff retry to the inventory client
context: The nightly order-sync job intermittently fails when the upstream inventory API returns a 503 under load. We confirmed it is transient: retrying the same request by hand succeeds within a second. The client in src/clients/inventory.js does a single fetch with no retry, so one 503 fails the whole sync and pages on-call. The codebase already has a backoff helper at src/lib/backoff.js used by the payments client; follow that pattern rather than adding a dependency. No caching layer is involved.
spec: Wrap the inventory fetch in src/clients/inventory.js with the existing withBackoff helper from src/lib/backoff.js: 3 attempts, exponential delay starting at 200ms, retry only on network errors and HTTP 502, 503, and 504. Leave the getInventory() signature unchanged so callers need no edits.

source_references.note: The single fetch call inside getInventory() that needs the retry wrapper.
Existing withBackoff helper to reuse; mirror how src/clients/payments.js calls it.

acceptance_criteria: A 503 from the inventory API is retried up to 3 times before the job fails.
A successful retry leaves no error in the nightly sync log.
The getInventory() signature is unchanged and existing callers are untouched.

verification.expected: all tests pass, including a new retries-on-503 case for the inventory client
the job completes after two simulated 503s with no thrown error

out_of_scope: Do not change the payments client.
Do not add a caching layer or a new HTTP dependency.
